neutral.linePrev: Oh the click click clack of the heavy black trains
neutral.lineNext: Oh the tick tock tick of the ticking time bomb
stanza: And it rained all night and washed the filth away
Down New York air conditioned drains
Oh the click click clack of the heavy black trains
A million engines in neutral
Oh the tick tock tick of the ticking time bomb
Fifty feet of concrete underground
One little leak becomes a lake
Says the tiny voice in my earpiece
So I give in to the rhythm, the click click clack
I'm too wasted to fight back
Oh tick tock goes the pendulum
On the old grandfather clock

nihilist.lineNext: My manic and I have no plans to move on
stanza: My nihilist, my happy man
My manic and I have no plans to move on
But birds are singing to calm us down
And birds are singing to calm us down

northern.linePrev: Just before our love got lost you said
northern.line: "I am as constant as a northern star"
northern.lineNext: And I said, "Constantly in the darkness
stanza: Just before our love got lost you said
"I am as constant as a northern star"
And I said, "Constantly in the darkness
Where's that at?
If you want me I'll be in the bar"

naughty.linePrev: You know, he put me at ease
naughty.line: And he loved me so naughty
naughty.lineNext: Made me weak in the knees
stanza: He tried hard to help me
You know, he put me at ease
And he loved me so naughty
Made me weak in the knees
Oh, I wish I had a river I could skate away on

net.linePrev: Thinking back down the roads to then
stanza: I fell in with some drifters
Cast upon a beach town
Winn Dixie cold cuts and highway hand me downs
And I wound up fixing dinner
For them and Boston Jim
I well up with affection
Thinking back down the roads to then
The nets were overflowing
In the Gulf of Mexico
They were overflowing in the refuge of the roads

nervous.line: And it made most people nervous
stanza: There was spring along the ditches
There were good times in the cities
Oh, radiant happiness
It was all so light and easy
'Til I started analyzing
And I brought on my old ways
A thunderhead of judgment was
Gathering in my gaze
And it made most people nervous
They just didn't wanna know
What I was seeing in the refuge of the roads

nylon.linePrev: And finds a lady in a Paris dress
nylon.lineNext: You read those books where luxury
stanza: A camera pans the cocktail hour
Behind a blind of potted palms
And finds a lady in a Paris dress
With runs in her nylons

partner.line: Behind a blind of potted palms

nylon.line: Mama's nylons underneath my cowgirl jeans
nylon.lineNext: He showed me first you get the kisses
stanza: And when I went skating after Golden Reggie
You know it was white lace I was chasing
Chasing dreams
Mama's nylons underneath my cowgirl jeans
He showed me first you get the kisses
And then you get the tears
But the ceremony of the bells and lace
Still veils this reckless fool here

noble.linePrev: Comes as a guest to take a slave
noble.line: Books where artists in noble poverty
noble.lineNext: Go like virgins to the grave
stanza: You read those books where luxury
Comes as a guest to take a slave
Books where artists in noble poverty
Go like virgins to the grave
Don't you get sensitive on me
'Cause I know you're just too proud
You couldn't step outside the Boho dance now
Even if good fortune allowed

noble.linePrev: Or put some time into ecology"
noble.line: Well, there's a wide wide world of noble causes
stanza: Dora says, "Have children"
Mama and Betsy say, "Find yourself a charity
Help the needy and the crippled
Or put some time into ecology"
Well, there's a wide wide world of noble causes
And lovely landscapes to discover
But all I really wanna do right now
Is find another lover

noisy.line: It's so noisy at the fair
noisy.lineNext: But all your friends are there
stanza: It's so noisy at the fair
But all your friends are there
And the candy floss you had
And your mother and your dad

noisy.linePrev: And the children let out from the schools
noisy.line: I was standing on a noisy corner
stanza: I was standing on a noisy corner
Waiting for the walking green
Across the street he stood
And he played real good
On his clarinet for free

needy.linePrev: Mama and Betsy say, "Find yourself a charity
needy.line: Help the needy and the crippled
needy.lineNext: Or put some time into ecology"
stanza: Dora says, "Have children"
Mama and Betsy say, "Find yourself a charity
Help the needy and the crippled
Or put some time into ecology"
Well, there's a wide wide world of noble causes
And lovely landscapes to discover
But all I really wanna do right now
Is find another lover

nеver.linePrev: All in all, I've been happy with my life
nеver.line: I was nеver really far from you, was I?
stanza: I was nеver really far from you, was I?
Something, somеthing, Caroline

notepad.linePrev: In case she’s got something she really really needs to say
notepad.line: She puts it in a notepad
notepad.lineNext: She's gonna write a book someday
stanza: She keeps a pen behind her ear
In case she’s got something she really really needs to say
She puts it in a notepad
She's gonna write a book someday

narcissist.linePrev: Woke up in a country who refused to hold your hand
narcissist.line: Kept falling for narcissists who insist you call them 'man'
narcissist.lineNext: You work late for a job you hate that's never fit the plan
stanza: Woke up in a country who refused to hold your hand
Kept falling for narcissists who insist you call them 'man'
You work late for a job you hate that's never fit the plan
Stay low, keep brave

naïve.linePrev: And you say she reminds you of me
naïve.line: Every little boy is so naïve
naïve.lineNext: I will not be a victim of romance
stanza: So your grandmother sounds to me
A woman I would be proud to be
And you say she reminds you of me
Every little boy is so naïve

naïve.linePrev: Head up, shoulders back, and proud to be
naïve.line: Every little girl is so naïve
naïve.lineNext: Falling in love with the first man she sees
stanza: So your grandfather sounds like me
Head up, shoulders back, and proud to be
Every little girl is so naïve
Falling in love with the first man she sees

naïve.lineNext: Asking what things you have seen
stanza: Oh, naïve little me
Asking what things you have seen
And you're vulnerable in your head
And you'll scream and you'll wail 'til you're dead

naivety.line: Thank you, naivety, for failing me again
stanza: You weren't my curse
You weren't my curse
Thank you, naivety, for failing me again
He was my next verse
